qa.question: Which statement best describes Harry's underlying approach to the various religious beliefs held by others?
option: A. He strictly enforces his own beliefs on everyone around him.
B. He believes all religions are inherently harmful and destructive to family unity.
C. He accepts and tolerates a wide variety of differing faiths within his own household without conflict.
D. He only respects beliefs that are rooted in ancient history and texts.
E. He ignores the topic of religion entirely because it causes too many arguments.

evidence_dialogues.speaker: Harry
evidence_dialogues.utterance: Edith, I was raised on the Torah... My wife, on the Quran. My oldest son is an Atheist. My youngest is a Scientologist. My daughter is studying Hinduism. I imagine that there is room there for a holy war in my living room. But we practice live and let live.

reasoning_steps.inference: Harry lists multiple contrasting religions practiced by his immediate family members.
His concluding statement 'we practice live and let live' demonstrates a stable, tolerant persona regarding diverse beliefs.

qa.question: How does Harry attempt to change the mood of the room early in the afternoon?

B. By suggesting they play a game and acting out a primal scenario.
C. By aggressively questioning Dan about his scientific theories.
D. By immediately asking John to reveal his true identity.
E. By leaving the room to check on Will outside.

evidence_dialogues.utterance: Charades?

evidence_dialogues.speaker: Harry
evidence_dialogues.utterance: (grunts like ape)

reasoning_steps.inference: Harry recognizes they have an afternoon to kill and suggests a game.
He immediately engages in play-acting an ape, indicating a playful short-term state.

evidence_dialogues.utterance: That's right, and they weren't the first, right?

reasoning_steps.inference: Dan explicitly states the commandments are updates of the more ancient Hammurabi's code.
Harry agrees with this sequence, confirming the temporal order of these codes.

qa.question: According to John's narrative, what is the sequence of regions he inhabited after meeting the Buddha?
option: A. India, then Tibet, then Rome.
B. The Mediterranean, then the near East, then central Europe.
C. Central Europe, then the Mediterranean, then New Guinea.
D. The near East, then India, then the Roman Empire.
E. Tibet, then the near East, then Brussels.

evidence_dialogues.speaker: John
evidence_dialogues.utterance: Guy met the Buddha, likes what he heard, thought about it for a while- Say 500 years, while he returned to the Mediterranean, became an Etruscan. Seeped into the Roman Empire. He didn't like what they became- A giant killing machine. He went to the near East thinking, "Why not pass the Buddha's teachings on in a modern form?"

evidence_dialogues.speaker: John
evidence_dialogues.utterance: Thus, I was resurrected, and I ascended to central Europe to get away as far as possible.

reasoning_steps.inference: After meeting the Buddha, John returned to the Mediterranean.
He then traveled to the near East to pass on the teachings.
Following the crucifixion event, he ascended to central Europe.

qa.question: What specific physiological techniques did John claim to employ during the crucifixion?
option: A. He used hallucinogenic herbs to induce a state of complete unconsciousness.
B. He blocked the pain and slowed his bodily processes until they were undetectable.
C. He convinced his captors to tie him instead of using nails, avoiding physical damage.
D. He relied on a mechanism allowing survival for thousands of years to rapidly heal his wounds.
E. He hypnotized the guards into believing he was dead while he escaped.

evidence_dialogues.speaker: John
evidence_dialogues.utterance: He blocked the pain, as he had learned to do in Tibet and India. He also learned to slow his body processes down to the point where they were undetectable. They thought he was dead.

reasoning_steps.inference: John details the exact actions he took to survive.
These actions specifically included blocking pain and slowing body processes to an undetectable level.

qa.question: How does John describe the nature of his brief relationship with the man he met in the 1600s?
option: A. They formed a lifelong partnership and traveled the world together.
B. They became bitter enemies after discovering their shared condition.
C. They conversed for a couple of days but parted with mutual uncertainty about each other's authenticity.
D. They immediately recognized each other as brothers and shared all their secrets.
E. They agreed to start a new religion together based on their longevity.

evidence_dialogues.speaker: John
evidence_dialogues.utterance: Anyway, he said yes, but from another time, another place. We talked for two days. It was all pretty convincing, but we couldn't be sure.

evidence_dialogues.speaker: John
evidence_dialogues.utterance: We each confirmed what the other said, but how do we know if the confirmation was genuine or an echo? ... He said he was inclined with the same reservation.

evidence_dialogues.speaker: John
evidence_dialogues.utterance: We parted, agreeing to keep in touch- But of course, we didn't-

reasoning_steps.inference: John and the man spoke for two days but maintained reservations about whether the other was playing a game.
They parted ways and failed to keep in touch, showing a brief and ultimately disconnected relationship.

qa.question: According to John, exactly how many years passed between his initial meeting with another supposed immortal and the time he thought he saw him again?

evidence_dialogues.speaker: John
evidence_dialogues.utterance: We parted, agreeing to keep in touch- But of course, we didn't- And 200 years later I thought I saw him in a train station in Brussels.

reasoning_steps.inference: John explicitly states the numerical gap in years before seeing the man again.

qa.question: Which statement best captures Dan's intellectual approach to John's extraordinary claims?
option: A. He completely rejects them as blasphemous and refuses to engage.

evidence_dialogues.speaker: Dan
evidence_dialogues.utterance: Think about it- A mechanism allowing survival for thousands of years?

evidence_dialogues.speaker: Dan
evidence_dialogues.utterance: Well then we'd have to go to Mars as a colony as we expanded, as we'd have to.

evidence_dialogues.speaker: Dan
evidence_dialogues.utterance: That, uh, raises an interesting question, John. Could there be others like you who escaped the aging process as you have?

reasoning_steps.inference: Dan engages with John's premise by posing theoretical questions about biology and expansion.
He analytically deduces the possibility of other immortals based on John's existence.

session_1.text: Scene 10: Jesus

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Will! I didn't know about Mary. I'm sorry. I can see how this might have hit you.

session_1.speaker: Will
session_1.text: Please, permit me to be infantile by myself.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: (holding out his hand, with the other hand on Will's arm)

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Will, please.

session_1.speaker: Will
session_1.text: (hands him the gun, turns around and walks away)

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: (checks gun to find it unloaded)

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Meanwhile, inside.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: What the hell were you thinking, Art?

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: Oh, come on. Something had to be done.

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: I have to say I agree.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: And he's our friend. Whatever else on earth is going on, he's our friend!

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: You sure about that?

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Why the hell are you being so hard on him, Edith?

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: One of my favorite people has disappeared. Can you get Alzheimer's at 35? Maybe I'm trying to wake him up. Maybe I'm too sad to cry.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: (entering)

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: What I said about myself hurt him. He struck back.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: Expertly- That stuff about stealing life forces?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: I've always wondered about the reasons.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Well, we still have an afternoon to kill, right?

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Charades?

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: (chuckling)

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: No. John? I have a charade, and it is just for you. A'right? Sandy, come here. Come on, come on, come on. Okay, this one's for you.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: A'right. Ready?

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: (grunts like ape)

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: (notices Sandy and wolf whistles)

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: (more grunting, play-acts whacking Sandy on head with club)

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Ooh! ...Unh.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: (puts arm around Sandy)

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: My first wedding?

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: There you go. There you go! Very good, and I bet at least one of us...

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: (points around the room)

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: ...is your direct descendent.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: And I didn't even send a Christmas card.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Christmas card? What about a birthday card? And don't even get me started on the candles... with the blowing and the...

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: (huffs)

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: ...and all the candles!

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Yeah, all right. I tried.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: Well, uh, call me underdeveloped, but I'd like to hear more.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Me too.

session_1.speaker: Linda
session_1.text: More.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: You double-damn swear this isn't some cockamamie science fiction story you're pulling on us?

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: Next question.

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: You- You- You realize this is an invitation to men in white suits with happy pills.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: Think about it- A mechanism allowing survival for thousands of years?

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: Run out of room even faster.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: Well then we'd have to go to Mars as a colony as we expanded, as we'd have to.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: I'd like that. On a planet of another star?

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: I envy you.

session_1.speaker: Linda
session_1.text: Did you have a pet dinosaur?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: (smiling)

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: They were a little bit before my time.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: At least something is.

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: No doubt you could give us a thousand details, John, corroborating your story, from the Magdalene to the Buddha to now.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Ten thousand, and you could stay out of the books.

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: Oh, it's getting chilly.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: (beckoning to the fireplace)

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Here, come over here. Join me.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: That, uh, raises an interesting question, John.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: Could there be others like you who escaped the aging process as you have?

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Representing something terrific we don't even know about biology.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: We're learning all the time.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Yeah, but how would he know? He doesn't wear an armband, an I.D. Badge saying "yabba dabba doo."

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: (chuckles)

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: There was a man in the 1600s.

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: (interrupting)

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: Where were you in 1292 A.D.?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Where were you a year ago on this date?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Anyway, it was the 1600s, and I met a man, and I had a hunch that he was...like me, so I told him.

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: Ah. See, you said this was a first.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: I forgot.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: A crack in your story, John?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: A touch of senility.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Anyway, he said yes, but from another time, another place. We talked for two days. It was all pretty convincing, but we couldn't be sure.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: We each confirmed what the other said, but how do we know if the confirmation was genuine or an echo? I knew I was legit, but I thought, "maybe he's playing a game on me." You know, a scholar of all we spoke about. He said he was inclined with the same reservation.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: Now, that's interesting. Just as we can never be sure, even if we wanted to- I mean, if we were sure, you couldn't be sure of that.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: We parted, agreeing to keep in touch- But of course, we didn't- And 200 years later I thought I saw him in a train station in Brussels. Lost him in the crowd.

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: Oh, what a shame.

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: I- I mean, if it were true.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Okay, here's one for you. What do you do in your spare time?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: (laughing)

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Oh every 50 years or so, when I wanna get away from the rush, I go down to a primitive tribe in New Guinea, Where I'm worshipped as an immortal god, and they have a huge statue of me. It's a big party. Yeah, I've got a lot of pictures of it, but I've already packed them up. I'm sorry.

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: I won't make the obvious nasty crack about more unwashed cavemen.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Actually, bathing was the style until the middle ages when the church told us it was sinful to wash away god's dirt, so people were sewn into their underwear in October and they popped out in April.

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: You said you just happened. I don't believe that. If your story's true, why did God allow you to happen?

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: That makes an interesting point. Are you religious, John?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: I don't follow a known religion. No.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: Ever?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Long time ago I did, like most people. Some just never get over it.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: Do you believe in god?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: As Laplace said, "I have no need of that hypothesis." He may be around, though.

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: He's everywhere. We just can't see him.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Pfft. If this were the best I could do, I'd be hiding, too.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: And creation...

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: It's here, I'm not so sure it was created.

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: What then?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Maybe it's just accumulated, fields affecting fields.

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: What about the source of the field energies?

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: Wouldn't that imply a prime mover?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: I'd wonder about the source of the prime mover, and turtles all the way down, infinite regress, but that doesn't imply anything to me.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: ...Back to the mystery.

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: It's a very old question, but there's no answer except in religious terms. If you have faith, it's answered.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: Did you ever meet any person from our religious history? A Biblical figure?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: In a way.

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: Who?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: We should skip this one.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: No, no, no skipping. Come on.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Next question.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: No, come on!

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Everyone clamoring.

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: Come on, spit it out!

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: Good lord! You were one of them!

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: This is going in a direction that I- I- I- I didn't expect. I hoped it wouldn't- We...should call it a night.

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: Come on! You were someone in religious history?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: (hesitates)

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Yes.

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: In the bible?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: (hesitates)

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Yes.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Someone we know?

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: How could we not know someone in the bible?

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: I mean somebody important.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: You may think you know him, but it's mostly myth.

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: (loudly)

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: The entire bible is...mostly m-myth and allegory with maybe some...basis in historical events.

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: Y-You...were part of that history?!

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Yes.

session_1.speaker: Linda
session_1.text: Moses.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Moses was based on Misis, a Syrian myth, and there are earlier versions- All found floating on water, the staff that changed to a snake, waters that were parted so followers could be led to freedom and even receive laws on stone or wooden tablets.

session_1.speaker: Linda
session_1.text: One of the apostles

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: They weren't really apostles. They didn't do any real teaching. Peter the fisherman learned a little more about fishing.

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: How do you know that?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: The mythical overlay is so enormous...and not good. The truth is so… so… simple.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: The New Testament in a hundred words or less.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: You ready?

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: I don't think I wanna hear this. Harry, will you take me home?

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: No! Not right now. I do want to hear this.

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: Sit down, Edith. You act like you believe him.

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: (softly)

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: It's sacrilege.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: How can it be sacrilege? He hasn't said anything yet.

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: (righteously)

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: The new New Testament is sacrilege.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: There are a dozen new new testaments, from Hebrew to Greek to Latin to Tyndale, all the way to King James, all revisionist, and all called revealed truth.

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: I mean a new New Testament in 100 words.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: I can give you the Ten Commandments in ten words: Don't. Don't, don't, don't. Don't, don't, don't, don't, don't, don't.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: (laughing)

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: Don't. The commandments are just modern updates of more ancient laws. Hammurabi's code.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: That's right, and they weren't the first, right?

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Edith, I was raised on the Torah... My wife, on the Quran. My oldest son is an Atheist. My youngest is a Scientologist. My daughter is studying Hinduism. I imagine that there is room there for a holy war in my living room. But we practice live and let live.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Why don't you sit down?

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Edith hesitantly sits

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: What is your preferred version of the bible?

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: The King James, of course. It's the most modern, the work of great scholars.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: Modern is good.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Alright, John, hit us with the short form.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Choral/religious music...

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Guy met the Buddha, likes what he heard, thought about it for a while- Say 500 years, while he returned to the Mediterranean, became an Etruscan. Seeped into the Roman Empire. He didn't like what they became- A giant killing machine. He went to the near East thinking, "Why not pass the Buddha's teachings on in a modern form?" So he tried. One dissident against Rome?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Rome won. The rest is history. Well, sort of.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Lot of fairy tales mixed in.

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: (quietly)

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: I knew it.

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: He's saying he was Christ.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Oh, no. That's the medal they pinned on Jesus to fulfill prophecy.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: The crucifixion.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: He blocked the pain, as he had learned to do in Tibet and India. He also learned to slow his body processes down to the point where they were undetectable. They thought he was dead. So his followers pulled him from the cross, placed him in a cave... His body normalized as he had trained it to... He attempted to go away undetected, but some devotees were standing watch. Tried to explain. They were ecstatic.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Thus, I was resurrected, and I ascended to central Europe to get away as far as possible.

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: You don't mean a word of this, John. My god, why are you doing this?!

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: Let me see your wrists.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: I don't scar. Besides, they tied me...but nails and blood make better religious Art.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: (laughing)

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: All the speculations about Jesus. He was black, he was Asian, he was a blue-eyed Aryan with a golden beard and hair straight out of Vidal Sassoon's, he was a benevolent alien, he never existed at all.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: ...Now he's a caveman.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: (laughs)

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: The Christ figure goes all the way back to Krishna- Hercules, of course.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Hercules?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Born of a virgin: Alcmene. A god for a father: Zeus. The only begotten. The savior- The Greek?

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: "Soter". The Good Shepherd, the Prince of Peace, bringing gentle persuasion and divine wisdom. He died, joined his father on Olympus a thousand years before Gethsemane.

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: How can you compare Pagan mythology to the true word?

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Pretty damn closely, I'd say.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: The early Christian leaders, they threw away Hebrew manuscripts and borrowed from Pagan sources all over the place.

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: Do you realize how...inconsiderately you're treating my feelings?

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: About as inconsiderately as we're treating John's.

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: Well, he does- he doesn't believe what he's saying!

session_1.speaker: Sandy
session_1.text: Do you believe literally everything in the bible, Edith?

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: Yes!

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: ...N- Uh- Before you say it, I know it's undergone a lot of changes, but God has spoken through man to make his word clearer.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: He couldn't get it right the first time?

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: We-we're imperfect! He had t-to work to make us understand.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: He couldn't get us right the first time, Edith?

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: Taken alone, the philosophical teachings of Jesus are Buddhism with a Hebrew accent- Kindness, tolerance, brotherhood, love... A ruthless realism acknowledging that life is as it is here on earth, here and now.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: The Kingdom of God, meaning...goodness, is...is right here, where it should be. "I am what I am becoming." That's what the Buddha brought in.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: And that's what I taught. But a talking snake make a lady eat an apple, so we're screwed.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Heaven and Hell were peddled so priests could rule through seduction and terror...save our souls that we never lost in the first place.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: I threw a clean pass...they ran it out of the ballpark.

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: This is blasphemy. It's horrible! Who else were you? Solomon? Elvis? Jack the Ripper?

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: It's been said that Buddha and Jesus would laugh or cry if they'd known what was done in their name.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: And if there is a creator, he'd probably feel the same way.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: I see ceremony, ritual, processions, genuflecting, moaning, intoning, venerating cookies and wine.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: And I think...it's not what I had in mind.

session_1.speaker: Edith
session_1.text: But that's Vatican flapdoodle. It doesn't have a thing to do with God.

session_1.speaker: Dan
session_1.text: As you said, John. Everywhere, religions...from exalting life to purging joy as a sin. Rome does it as grand opera. A simple path to goodness needs a supernatural roadmap.

session_1.speaker: Harry
session_1.text: Supernatural...

session_1.speaker: Art
session_1.text: A stupid word, I mean...anything that happens, happens within nature, whether we believe in it or not.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: (looks up)

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: ...Like a 14,000-year-old caveman.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: The sound of a car approaching...

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: John heads outside to receive Will.

session_1.speaker: Will
session_1.text: I--I--I drove for a while, and then I sat for a while. I'm so ashamed.

session_1.speaker: Will
session_1.text: (Shivering)

session_1.speaker: Will
session_1.text: And I'm freezing!

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Well, come inside.

session_1.speaker: Will
session_1.text: I still don't believe you, of course. You need help.

session_1.speaker: John
session_1.text: Everybody needs help.

session_1.speaker: Will
session_1.text: Yes, well, some more than others.

session_1.speaker: Will
session_1.text: Fade out.
